 29	/**
 30	 * @brief GLFW keyboard callback function for handling global key events
 31	 *
 32	 * Handles application-wide keyboard input, specifically for closing the window
 33	 * when ESC or Q keys are pressed. This callback is registered with GLFW and
 34	 * called automatically when key events occur.
 35	 *
 36	 * @param window Pointer to the GLFW window that received the event
 37	 * @param key The keyboard key that was pressed or released
 38	 * @param scancode The system-specific scancode of the key
 39	 * @param action GLFW_PRESS, GLFW_RELEASE or GLFW_REPEAT
 40	 * @param mods Bit field describing which modifier keys were held down
 41	 */
 42	void keyCallback(GLFWwindow* window, const int key, int scancode, const int action, int mods) {
 43	 // Close window on ESC, Q, or q
 44	 if (key == GLFW_KEY_ESCAPE || key == GLFW_KEY_Q) {
 45	 if (action == GLFW_PRESS) {
 46	 glfwSetWindowShouldClose(window, GL_TRUE);
 47	 }
 48	 }
 49	}
 50	
 51	/**
 52	 * @brief Restarts the game by resetting all game components to their initial state
 53	 *
 54	 * This function performs a complete game reset, including:
 55	 * - Resetting ball position and physics (velocity, orientation)
 56	 * - Regenerating the labyrinth with a new layout
 57	 * - Resetting the rotation controller
 58	 * - Hiding the victory screen and returning to playing state
 59	 *
 60	 * The function ensures all game objects are properly cleaned up and reinitialized
 61	 * to provide a fresh game experience without memory leaks.
 62	 *
 63	 * @param ball Reference to the Ball object to reset
 64	 * @param maze Reference to pointer to Labyrinth (will be deleted and recreated)
 65	 * @param rotator Reference to Rotation object to reset
 66	 * @param victoryScreen Reference to VictoryScreen object to reset
 67	 * @param gameState Reference to GameStateManager to transition back to playing
 68	 */
 69	void restartGame(Ball& ball, Labyrinth*& maze, Rotation& rotator, VictoryScreen& victoryScreen, GameStateManager& gameState) {
 70	 // Reset ball position to top right corner within floor grid bounds
 71	 ball.setPosition(glm::vec3(12.0f, 12.0f, ball.getRadius()));
 72	 ball.resetPhysics(); // Reset velocity, orientation, etc.
 73	
 74	 // Reset labyrinth rotation
 75	 rotator = Rotation(MAX_ROTATION_SPEED);
 76	
 77	 // Generate new maze
 78	 delete maze;
 79	 maze = new Labyrinth();
 80	 maze->createPlayfield();
 81	
 82	 // Reset victory screen and game state
 83	 victoryScreen.reset();
 84	 gameState.startPlaying();
 85	}

 42	void keyCallback(GLFWwindow* window, const int key, int scancode, const int action, int mods) {

 46	 glfwSetWindowShouldClose(window, GL_TRUE);
 47	 }
 48	 }

 126	void OBD2Manager::initializeOBD2() {
 127	 if (millis() - lastInitCommand < INIT_COMMAND_DELAY) {
 128	 return; // Wait for delay between commands
 129	 }
 130	
 131	 String commands[] = {"ATZ", "ATE0", "ATL0", "ATS0", "ATH0", "ATSP0"};
 132	 String descriptions[] = {"Reset", "Echo off", "Linefeeds off", "Spaces off", "Headers off", "Auto protocol"};
 133	
 134	 if (initStep < 6) {
 135	 Serial.println("Init step " + String(initStep) + ": " + descriptions[initStep]);
 136	 sendCommand(commands[initStep]);
 137	 lastInitCommand = millis();
 138	 initStep++;
 139	 } else {
 140	 Serial.println("OBD2 initialization complete");
 141	 currentState = OBD2State::READY;
 142	 }
 143	}

 145	float OBD2Manager::parseCoolantTemp(const String& response) {
 146	 // Expected response format: "4105XX" where XX is the temperature value
 147	 if (response.length() >= 6 && response.substring(0, 4) == "4105") {
 148	 String hexValue = response.substring(4, 6);
 149	 int tempValue = strtol(hexValue.c_str(), NULL, 16);
 150	 float temperature = tempValue - 40; // Convert to Celsius
 151	
 152	 // Validate reasonable temperature range
 153	 if (temperature >= -40 && temperature <= 150) {
 154	 return temperature;
 155	 }
 156	 }
 157	 return -999; // Invalid data
 158	}
 159	
 160	float OBD2Manager::parseBoostPressure(const String& response) {
 161	 // Expected response format: "410BXX" where XX is the pressure value
 162	 if (response.length() >= 6 && response.substring(0, 4) == "410B") {
 163	 String hexValue = response.substring(4, 6);
 164	 int pressureValue = strtol(hexValue.c_str(), NULL, 16);
 165	 float pressure = pressureValue; // Value is already in kPa
 166	
 167	 // Validate reasonable pressure range
 168	 if (pressure >= 0 && pressure <= 300) {
 169	 return pressure;
 170	 }
 171	 }
 172	 return -999; // Invalid data
 173	}
 174	
 175	bool OBD2Manager::isValidResponse(const String& response) {
 176	 // Check for common error responses
 177	 if (response.length() < 4) return false;
 178	 if (response.indexOf("NODATA") >= 0) return false;
 179	 if (response.indexOf("ERROR") >= 0) return false;
 180	 if (response.indexOf("?") >= 0) return false;
 181	
 182	 return true;
 183	}
 184	 case OBD2State::INITIALIZING: return "Initializing";
 185	 case OBD2State::READY: return "Ready";
 186	 case OBD2State::ERROR: return "Error";
 187	 default: return "Unknown";

 9	#endif
 10	
 11	// TFT Display setup
 12	TFT_eSPI tft = TFT_eSPI();

 14	// Bluetooth Serial setup

 16	
 17	// OBD2 Variables
 18	String obd2Response = "";
 19	bool bluetoothConnected = false;
 20	bool obd2Initialized = false;
 21	
 22	// Sensor data
 23	float coolantTemp = 0.0; // Celsius
 24	float boostPressure = 0.0; // kPa
 25	
 26	// Timing variables
 27	unsigned long lastUpdate = 0;

 29	
 30	// Display constants
 31	const int SCREEN_WIDTH = 240;
 32	const int SCREEN_HEIGHT = 320;
 33	const int GAUGE_RADIUS = 80;
 34	const int GAUGE_CENTER_Y = 100;
 35	const int TEMP_GAUGE_X = 120;
 36	const int BOOST_GAUGE_X = 120;
 37	const int TEMP_GAUGE_Y = 100;
 38	const int BOOST_GAUGE_Y = 220;
 39	
 40	// Color definitions
 41	#define COLOR_BACKGROUND TFT_BLACK
 42	#define COLOR_GAUGE_OUTLINE TFT_WHITE
 43	#define COLOR_NEEDLE TFT_RED
 44	#define COLOR_GREEN_ZONE TFT_GREEN
 45	#define COLOR_YELLOW_ZONE TFT_YELLOW
 46	#define COLOR_RED_ZONE TFT_RED
 47	#define COLOR_TEXT TFT_WHITE
 48	#define COLOR_TICK TFT_LIGHTGREY
 49	
 50	// Function declarations
 51	void initializeBluetooth();
 52	void initializeOBD2();
 53	void sendOBD2Command(String command);
 54	String readOBD2Response();
 55	void requestSensorData();
 56	float parseCoolantTemp(String response);
 57	float parseBoostPressure(String response);
 58	void drawGauges();
 59	void drawAnalogGauge(int centerX, int centerY, int radius, float value, float minVal, float maxVal,
 60	 String title, String unit, uint16_t needleColor);
 61	void drawGaugeTicks(int centerX, int centerY, int radius, float minVal, float maxVal, int numTicks);
 62	void drawColorZones(int centerX, int centerY, int radius, float minVal, float maxVal,
 63	 float greenMax, float yellowMax);

 66	void setup() {
 67	 Serial.begin(115200);
 68	 Serial.println("ESP32 OBD2 Gauge Display Starting...");
 69	
 70	 // Initialize TFT display
 71	 tft.init();
 72	 tft.setRotation(0); // Portrait mode
 73	 tft.fillScreen(COLOR_BACKGROUND);
 74	 tft.setTextColor(COLOR_TEXT);
 75	 tft.setTextSize(2);
 76	 tft.drawString("Initializing...", 10, 10);
 77	
 78	 // Initialize Bluetooth
 79	 initializeBluetooth();
 80	
 81	 // Draw initial gauges
 82	 drawGauges();
 83	
 84	 Serial.println("Setup complete!");
 85	}
 86	
 87	void loop() {
 88	 // Check if it's time to update sensor data
 89	 if (millis() - lastUpdate >= updateInterval) {
 90	 if (bluetoothConnected && obd2Initialized) {
 91	 requestSensorData();
 92	 drawGauges();
 93	 } else if (bluetoothConnected && !obd2Initialized) {
 94	 initializeOBD2();
 95	 }
 96	 lastUpdate = millis();
 97	 }
 98	
 99	 // Handle Bluetooth connection status
 100	 if (SerialBT.hasClient()) {
 101	 if (!bluetoothConnected) {
 102	 bluetoothConnected = true;
 103	 Serial.println("Bluetooth client connected");
 104	 tft.fillRect(0, 0, SCREEN_WIDTH, 30, COLOR_BACKGROUND);
 105	 tft.drawString("BT Connected", 10, 10);
 106	 }
 107	 } else {
 108	 if (bluetoothConnected) {
 109	 bluetoothConnected = false;
 110	 obd2Initialized = false;
 111	 Serial.println("Bluetooth client disconnected");
 112	 tft.fillRect(0, 0, SCREEN_WIDTH, 30, COLOR_BACKGROUND);
 113	 tft.drawString("BT Disconnected", 10, 10);
 114	 }
 115	 }
 116	
 117	 delay(100); // Small delay to prevent excessive CPU usage
 118	}
 119	
 120	void initializeBluetooth() {
 121	 SerialBT.begin("ESP32_OBD2_Display"); // Bluetooth device name
 122	 Serial.println("Bluetooth initialized. Waiting for connection...");
 123	 tft.fillRect(0, 0, SCREEN_WIDTH, 30, COLOR_BACKGROUND);
 124	 tft.drawString("Waiting for BT...", 10, 10);
 125	}
 126	
 127	void initializeOBD2() {
 128	 Serial.println("Initializing OBD2...");
 129	
 130	 // Send initialization commands
 131	 sendOBD2Command("ATZ"); // Reset
 132	 delay(1000);
 133	 sendOBD2Command("ATE0"); // Echo off
 134	 delay(500);
 135	 sendOBD2Command("ATL0"); // Linefeeds off
 136	 delay(500);
 137	 sendOBD2Command("ATS0"); // Spaces off
 138	 delay(500);
 139	 sendOBD2Command("ATH0"); // Headers off
 140	 delay(500);
 141	 sendOBD2Command("ATSP0"); // Set protocol to auto
 142	 delay(1000);
 143	
 144	 obd2Initialized = true;
 145	 Serial.println("OBD2 initialized successfully");
 146	 tft.fillRect(0, 0, SCREEN_WIDTH, 30, COLOR_BACKGROUND);
 147	 tft.drawString("OBD2 Ready", 10, 10);
 148	}
 149	
 150	void sendOBD2Command(String command) {
 151	 if (SerialBT.hasClient()) {
 152	 SerialBT.println(command);
 153	 Serial.println("Sent: " + command);
 154	 }
 155	}
 156	
 157	String readOBD2Response() {
 158	 String response = "";
 159	 unsigned long timeout = millis() + 3000; // 3 second timeout
 160	
 161	 while (millis() < timeout) {
 162	 if (SerialBT.available()) {
 163	 char c = SerialBT.read();
 164	 if (c == '\r' || c == '\n') {
 165	 if (response.length() > 0) {
 166	 break;
 167	 }
 168	 } else {
 169	 response += c;
 170	 }
 171	 }
 172	 delay(10);
 173	 }
 174	
 175	 Serial.println("Received: " + response);
 176	 return response;
 177	}
 178	
 179	void requestSensorData() {
 180	 // Request coolant temperature (PID 0105)
 181	 sendOBD2Command("0105");
 182	 delay(100);
 183	 String tempResponse = readOBD2Response();
 184	 if (tempResponse.length() > 0) {
 185	 coolantTemp = parseCoolantTemp(tempResponse);
 186	 }
 187	
 188	 delay(200);
 189	
 190	 // Request intake manifold pressure (PID 010B)
 191	 sendOBD2Command("010B");
 192	 delay(100);
 193	 String pressureResponse = readOBD2Response();
 194	 if (pressureResponse.length() > 0) {
 195	 boostPressure = parseBoostPressure(pressureResponse);
 196	 }
 197	}
 198	
 199	float parseCoolantTemp(String response) {
 200	 // Expected response format: "41 05 XX" where XX is the temperature value
 201	 if (response.length() >= 6 && response.substring(0, 4) == "4105") {
 202	 String hexValue = response.substring(4, 6);
 203	 int tempValue = strtol(hexValue.c_str(), NULL, 16);
 204	 float temperature = tempValue - 40; // Convert to Celsius
 205	 Serial.println("Coolant Temperature: " + String(temperature) + "°C");
 206	 return temperature;
 207	 }
 208	 return coolantTemp; // Return previous value if parsing fails
 209	}
 210	
 211	float parseBoostPressure(String response) {
 212	 // Expected response format: "41 0B XX" where XX is the pressure value
 213	 if (response.length() >= 6 && response.substring(0, 4) == "410B") {
 214	 String hexValue = response.substring(4, 6);
 215	 int pressureValue = strtol(hexValue.c_str(), NULL, 16);
 216	 float pressure = pressureValue; // Value is already in kPa
 217	 Serial.println("Boost Pressure: " + String(pressure) + " kPa");
 218	 return pressure;
 219	 }
 220	 return boostPressure; // Return previous value if parsing fails
 221	}
 222	void drawGauges() {
 223	 // Clear the gauge areas
 224	 tft.fillRect(0, 40, SCREEN_WIDTH, SCREEN_HEIGHT - 40, COLOR_BACKGROUND);
 225	
 226	 // Draw coolant temperature gauge (40°C to 120°C)
 227	 drawAnalogGauge(TEMP_GAUGE_X, TEMP_GAUGE_Y, GAUGE_RADIUS, coolantTemp, 40.0, 120.0,
 228	 "Coolant", "C", COLOR_NEEDLE);
 229	
 230	 // Draw boost pressure gauge (90 kPa to 250 kPa)
 231	 drawAnalogGauge(BOOST_GAUGE_X, BOOST_GAUGE_Y, GAUGE_RADIUS, boostPressure, 90.0, 250.0,
 232	 "Boost", "kPa", COLOR_NEEDLE);
 233	}
 234	
 235	void drawAnalogGauge(int centerX, int centerY, int radius, float value, float minVal, float maxVal,
 236	 String title, String unit, uint16_t needleColor) {
 237	
 238	 // Draw color zones first (background)
 239	 if (title == "Coolant") {
 240	 drawColorZones(centerX, centerY, radius - 10, minVal, maxVal, 90.0, 105.0); // Green up to 90°C, Yellow to 105°C, Red above
 241	 } else if (title == "Boost") {
 242	 drawColorZones(centerX, centerY, radius - 10, minVal, maxVal, 180.0, 220.0); // Green up to 180kPa, Yellow to 220kPa, Red above
 243	 }
 244	
 245	 // Draw gauge outline
 246	 tft.drawCircle(centerX, centerY, radius, COLOR_GAUGE_OUTLINE);
 247	 tft.drawCircle(centerX, centerY, radius - 1, COLOR_GAUGE_OUTLINE);
 248	
 249	 // Draw tick marks
 250	 drawGaugeTicks(centerX, centerY, radius, minVal, maxVal, 8);
 251	
 252	 // Draw title
 253	 tft.setTextSize(1);
 254	 tft.setTextColor(COLOR_TEXT);
 255	 int titleWidth = title.length() * 6;
 256	 tft.drawString(title, centerX - titleWidth/2, centerY - radius - 20);
 257	
 258	 // Draw current value
 259	 String valueStr = String(value, 1) + unit;
 260	 tft.setTextSize(2);
 261	 int valueWidth = valueStr.length() * 12;
 262	 tft.drawString(valueStr, centerX - valueWidth/2, centerY + radius + 10);
 263	
 264	 // Calculate needle angle (180 degrees sweep, from -90 to +90 degrees)
 265	 float angle = mapFloat(value, minVal, maxVal, -90.0, 90.0);
 266	 angle = constrain(angle, -90.0, 90.0);
 267	
 268	 // Convert to radians
 269	 float angleRad = (angle + 90) * PI / 180.0; // Offset by 90 degrees and convert to radians
 270	
 271	 // Calculate needle end point
 272	 int needleLength = radius - 15;
 273	 int needleX = centerX + needleLength * cos(angleRad);
 274	 int needleY = centerY + needleLength * sin(angleRad);
 275	
 276	 // Draw needle
 277	 tft.drawLine(centerX, centerY, needleX, needleY, needleColor);
 278	 tft.drawLine(centerX + 1, centerY, needleX + 1, needleY, needleColor);
 279	 tft.drawLine(centerX, centerY + 1, needleX, needleY + 1, needleColor);
 280	
 281	 // Draw center dot
 282	 tft.fillCircle(centerX, centerY, 3, needleColor);
 283	}
 284	
 285	void drawGaugeTicks(int centerX, int centerY, int radius, float minVal, float maxVal, int numTicks) {
 286	 for (int i = 0; i <= numTicks; i++) {
 287	 float angle = mapFloat(i, 0, numTicks, -90.0, 90.0);
 288	 float angleRad = (angle + 90) * PI / 180.0;
 289	
 290	 int outerX = centerX + (radius - 5) * cos(angleRad);
 291	 int outerY = centerY + (radius - 5) * sin(angleRad);
 292	 int innerX = centerX + (radius - 15) * cos(angleRad);
 293	 int innerY = centerY + (radius - 15) * sin(angleRad);
 294	
 295	 tft.drawLine(outerX, outerY, innerX, innerY, COLOR_TICK);
 296	
 297	 // Draw tick labels
 298	 if (i % 2 == 0) { // Only label every other tick
 299	 float tickValue = mapFloat(i, 0, numTicks, minVal, maxVal);
 300	 String tickLabel = String((int)tickValue);
 301	
 302	 int labelX = centerX + (radius - 25) * cos(angleRad);
 303	 int labelY = centerY + (radius - 25) * sin(angleRad);
 304	
 305	 tft.setTextSize(1);
 306	 tft.setTextColor(COLOR_TEXT);
 307	 int labelWidth = tickLabel.length() * 6;
 308	 tft.drawString(tickLabel, labelX - labelWidth/2, labelY - 4);
 309	 }
 310	 }
 311	}
 312	
 313	void drawColorZones(int centerX, int centerY, int radius, float minVal, float maxVal,
 314	 float greenMax, float yellowMax) {
 315	
 316	 // Draw colored arcs for different zones
 317	 int numSegments = 60;
 318	
 319	 for (int i = 0; i < numSegments; i++) {
 320	 float angle1 = mapFloat(i, 0, numSegments, -90.0, 90.0);
 321	 float angle2 = mapFloat(i + 1, 0, numSegments, -90.0, 90.0);
 322	
 323	 float value = mapFloat(i, 0, numSegments, minVal, maxVal);
 324	
 325	 uint16_t color;
 326	 if (value <= greenMax) {
 327	 color = COLOR_GREEN_ZONE;
 328	 } else if (value <= yellowMax) {
 329	 color = COLOR_YELLOW_ZONE;
 330	 } else {
 331	 color = COLOR_RED_ZONE;
 332	 }
 333	
 334	 // Draw arc segment
 335	 float angleRad1 = (angle1 + 90) * PI / 180.0;
 336	 float angleRad2 = (angle2 + 90) * PI / 180.0;
 337	
 338	 int x1 = centerX + radius * cos(angleRad1);
 339	 int y1 = centerY + radius * sin(angleRad1);
 340	 int x2 = centerX + radius * cos(angleRad2);
 341	 int y2 = centerY + radius * sin(angleRad2);
 342	
 343	 tft.drawLine(x1, y1, x2, y2, color);
 344	 }
 345	}
 346	
 347	float mapFloat(float x, float in_min, float in_max, float out_min, float out_max) {
 348	 return (x - in_min) * (out_max - out_min) / (in_max - in_min) + out_min;
 349	}

 156	void GaugeDisplay::drawColorZones(const GaugeConfig& config) {
 157	 // Draw colored arcs for different zones
 158	 int numSegments = 60;
 159	
 160	 for (int i = 0; i < numSegments; i++) {
 161	 float angle1 = mapFloat(i, 0, numSegments, -90.0, 90.0);
 162	 float angle2 = mapFloat(i + 1, 0, numSegments, -90.0, 90.0);
 163	
 164	 float value = mapFloat(i, 0, numSegments, config.minValue, config.maxValue);
 165	
 166	 uint16_t color;
 167	 if (value <= config.greenThreshold) {
 168	 color = COLOR_GREEN_ZONE;
 169	 } else if (value <= config.yellowThreshold) {
 170	 color = COLOR_YELLOW_ZONE;
 171	 } else {
 172	 color = COLOR_RED_ZONE;
 173	 }
 174	
 175	 // Draw arc segment
 176	 float angleRad1 = (angle1 + 90) * PI / 180.0;
 177	 float angleRad2 = (angle2 + 90) * PI / 180.0;
 178	
 179	 int x1 = config.centerX + (config.radius - 10) * cos(angleRad1);
 180	 int y1 = config.centerY + (config.radius - 10) * sin(angleRad1);
 181	 int x2 = config.centerX + (config.radius - 10) * cos(angleRad2);
 182	 int y2 = config.centerY + (config.radius - 10) * sin(angleRad2);
 183	
 184	 tft.drawLine(x1, y1, x2, y2, color);
 185	 }
 186	}
 187	
 188	void GaugeDisplay::drawNeedle(const GaugeConfig& config, float value) {
 189	 // Calculate needle angle (180 degrees sweep, from -90 to +90 degrees)
 190	 float angle = mapFloat(value, config.minValue, config.maxValue, -90.0, 90.0);
 191	 angle = constrain(angle, -90.0, 90.0);
 192	
 193	 // Convert to radians
 194	 float angleRad = (angle + 90) * PI / 180.0; // Offset by 90 degrees and convert to radians
 195	
 196	 // Calculate needle end point
 197	 int needleLength = config.radius - 15;
 198	 int needleX = config.centerX + needleLength * cos(angleRad);
 199	 int needleY = config.centerY + needleLength * sin(angleRad);
 200	
 201	 // Draw needle (multiple lines for thickness)
 202	 tft.drawLine(config.centerX, config.centerY, needleX, needleY, config.needleColor);
 203	 tft.drawLine(config.centerX + 1, config.centerY, needleX + 1, needleY, config.needleColor);
 204	 tft.drawLine(config.centerX, config.centerY + 1, needleX, needleY + 1, config.needleColor);
 205	}
 206	
 207	float GaugeDisplay::mapFloat(float x, float in_min, float in_max, float out_min, float out_max) {
 208	 return (x - in_min) * (out_max - out_min) / (in_max - in_min) + out_min;
 209	}

 196	void OBD2Manager::sendCommand(const String& command) {
 197	 if (serialBT.hasClient()) {
 198	 serialBT.println(command);
 199	 Serial.println("Sent: " + command);
 200	 }
 201	}
 202	
 203	String OBD2Manager::readResponse(unsigned long timeout) {
 204	 String response = "";
 205	 unsigned long startTime = millis();
 206	
 207	 while (millis() - startTime < timeout) {
 208	 if (serialBT.available()) {
 209	 char c = serialBT.read();
 210	 if (c == '\r' || c == '\n') {
 211	 if (response.length() > 0) {
 212	 break;
 213	 }
 214	 } else if (c != ' ') { // Remove spaces
 215	 response += c;
 216	 }
 217	 }
 218	 delay(10);
 219	 }
 220	
 221	 Serial.println("Received: " + response);
 222	 return response;
 223	}

Renode 1.15.3
=============

[Latest](/renode/renode/releases/latest)

[Latest](/renode/renode/releases/latest)

Compare

Choose a tag to compare

Loading

[View all tags](/renode/renode/tags)

![@PiotrZierhoffer](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/1142698?s=40&v=4) [PiotrZierhoffer](/PiotrZierhoffer) released this 18 Sep 12:20

· [849 commits](/renode/renode/compare/v1.15.3...master) to master since this release

[v1.15.3](/renode/renode/tree/v1.15.3)

[`03756cb`](/renode/renode/commit/03756cb5a3112fb94aeffef227ddec48db531cad)

For installation instructions, see the [README](https://github.com/renode/renode/#installation).

Added and improved architecture support:

* fixed Arm MPU skipping access checks for MPU regions sharing a page with a background region
* FPU dirty flag is now set on all FPU load instructions for RISC-V
* fixed Arm PMSAv8 not checking for domains not being page aligned
* RISC-V MTVAL register now contains the invalid instruction after illegal instruction exception
* Arm SRS (Store Return State) instruction now saves onto stack SPSR instead of masked CPSR
* improved support for x86-64, verified with Zephyr
* added SMEPMP extension stub for RISC-V
* added ability to configure usable bits in RISC-V PMPADDR registers
* fixed runtime configurability of the RISC-V MISA registers
* fixed RISC-V PMPCFG semantics from WIRI to WARL
* fixed decoding of C.ADDI4SPN in RISC-V
* fixed behavior of RORIW, RORI and SLLI.UW RISC-V instructions
* changed MSTATUS RISC-V CSR to be more responsive to the presence of User and Supervisor modes

Added and improved platform descriptions:

* NXP MR-CANHUBK3
* NXP S32K388
* NXP S32K118
* RI5CY
* Renesas r7fa8m1a
* Renesas DA14592
* STM32H743
* x86-64 ACRN

Added demos and tests:

* Zephyr running hello\_world demo on x86-64 ACRN
* ZynqMP demo showcasing two way communication between Cortex-A53 running Linux and Cortex-R5 running OpenAMP echo sample

Added features:

* Socket Manager mechanism, organizing socket management in a single entity
* test real-time timeout handling mechanism in Robot
* GPIO events support for the External Control API
* Zephyr Mode support for Arm, Arm-M, SPARC, x86 and Xtensa
* disassembling support for x86-64 architecture
* support for bus access widths other than DoubleWord for DPI integration of APB3
* support for overriding a default implementation of the verilated UART model

Changed:

* improved `renesas-segger-rtt.py` helper
* Renode logs a warning instead of crashing when HDL co-simulated block reports an error
* improved `guest cache` tool results readability

Fixed:

* PulseGenerator behavior when `onTicks == offTicks`
* External Control API GetTime command returning incorrect results
* SystemC integration crashing when initializing GPIO connections
* USB Speed value reported in USB/IP device descriptor
* USB endpoints with the same number but opposite direction not being distinguished
* a potential crash due to `OverflowException` when stopping the emulation
* checking address range when mapping memory ranges in TranslationCPU
* configuration descriptor parsing in USBIPServer
* fatal TCG errors in some cases of invalid RISC-V instructions
* handling registration of regions not defined by peripherals
* handling registration of regions with unpaired access method
* incorrect sequence number in USBIP setup packet reply
* SD card reset condition
* starting GDB stub on platforms containing CPUs not supporting GDB
* infinite loop on debug exception with an interrupt pending
* simulation elements unpausing after some Monitor commands

Added peripheral models:

* Arm CoreLink Network Interconnect
* LPC Clock0
* RenesasDA14 GeneralPurposeRegisters
* STM32 SDMMC
* Synopsys SSI

Improvements in peripherals:

* Arm Signals Unit
* CAES ADC
* Gaisler FaultTolerantMemoryController
* LPC USART
* MiV CoreUART
* NXP LPUART
* RenesasDA Watchdog
* RenesasDA14 ClockGenerationController
* RISC-V Platform Level Interrupt Controller
* STM32 DMA
* ZynqMP IPI
* ZynqMP Platform Management Unit

Assets 12

 </return-code>
 <stdout>
 #!/bin/sh
REQUIRED_MAJOR=5
REQUIRED_MINOR=20

#get the bundle's MacOS directory full path
DIR=`dirname $0`

EXE_PATH="$DIR/bin/Renode.exe"
PROCESS_NAME=appname
APPNAME="Renode"

#set up environment
MONO_FRAMEWORK_PATH=/Library/Frameworks/Mono.framework/Versions/Current
export DYLD_FALLBACK_LIBRARY_PATH="$DIR:$MONO_FRAMEWORK_PATH/lib:/lib:/usr/lib"
export PATH="$MONO_FRAMEWORK_PATH/bin:$PATH"

# REQUIRED_MAJOR and REQUIRED_MINOR should be
# set automatically at the moment of packaging
if [ -z "$REQUIRED_MAJOR" -o -z "$REQUIRED_MINOR" ]
then
	echo "No required mono version set. It most probably indicates a problem in packaging scripts"
	exit 1
fi

VERSION_TITLE="Cannot launch $APPNAME"
VERSION_MSG="$APPNAME requires the Mono Framework version $REQUIRED_MAJOR.$REQUIRED_MINOR or later."
DOWNLOAD_URL="http://www.mono-project.com/download/stable/"

MONO_VERSION="$(mono64 --version | grep 'Mono JIT compiler version ' | cut -f5 -d\ )"
MONO_VERSION_MAJOR="$(echo $MONO_VERSION | cut -f1 -d.)"
MONO_VERSION_MINOR="$(echo $MONO_VERSION | cut -f2 -d.)"
if [ -z "$MONO_VERSION" ] \
	|| [ $MONO_VERSION_MAJOR -lt $REQUIRED_MAJOR ] \
	|| [ $MONO_VERSION_MAJOR -eq $REQUIRED_MAJOR -a $MONO_VERSION_MINOR -lt $REQUIRED_MINOR ]
then
	osascript \
	-e "set question to display dialog \"$VERSION_MSG\" with title \"$VERSION_TITLE\" buttons {\"Cancel\", \"Download...\"} default button 2" \
	-e "if button returned of question is equal to \"Download...\" then open location \"$DOWNLOAD_URL\""
	echo "$VERSION_TITLE"
	echo "$VERSION_MSG"
	exit 1
fi

#run app using mono
exec -a \"$PROCESS_NAME\" mono64 $MONO_OPTIONS "$EXE_PATH" "$@"

 </stdout>
 <stderr>
 
 </stderr>","isError":false,"requestId":""}},"aa9230de-16c4-45d1-a3e2-f3a4f5d981da;toolu_vrtx_01YQFVSgJtprTs9xMdZ5Mabh":{"requestId":"aa9230de-16c4-45d1-a3e2-f3a4f5d981da","toolUseId":"toolu_vrtx_01YQFVSgJtprTs9xMdZ5Mabh","phase":5,"result":{"text":"File saved.  156	void GaugeDisplay::drawColorZones(const GaugeConfig& config) {
 157	 // Draw colored arcs for different zones
 158	 int numSegments = 60;
 159	
 160	 for (int i = 0; i < numSegments; i++) {
 161	 float angle1 = mapFloat(i, 0, numSegments, -90.0, 90.0);
 162	 float angle2 = mapFloat(i + 1, 0, numSegments, -90.0, 90.0);
 163	
 164	 float value = mapFloat(i, 0, numSegments, config.minValue, config.maxValue);
 165	
 166	 uint16_t color;
 167	 if (value <= config.greenThreshold) {
 168	 color = COLOR_GREEN_ZONE;
 169	 } else if (value <= config.yellowThreshold) {
 170	 color = COLOR_YELLOW_ZONE;
 171	 } else {
 172	 color = COLOR_RED_ZONE;
 173	 }
 174	
 175	 // Draw arc segment
 176	 float angleRad1 = (angle1 + 90) * PI / 180.0;
 177	 float angleRad2 = (angle2 + 90) * PI / 180.0;
 178	
 179	 int x1 = config.centerX + (config.radius - 10) * cos(angleRad1);
 180	 int y1 = config.centerY + (config.radius - 10) * sin(angleRad1);
 181	 int x2 = config.centerX + (config.radius - 10) * cos(angleRad2);
 182	 int y2 = config.centerY + (config.radius - 10) * sin(angleRad2);
 183	
 184	 tft.drawLine(x1, y1, x2, y2, color);
 185	 }
 186	}

> 56	1. **Coolant Temperature Gauge**
 57	 - Range: 40°C to 130°C
 58	 - Special behavior: Needle stays at 0% below 40°C
 59	 - Always displays actual temperature value
 60	 - Color zones: Green (40-90°C), Yellow (90-105°C), Red (105°C+)
 61	
 62	2. **Turbo Boost Pressure Gauge**
 63	 - Range: 0.9 to 2.5 bar
 64	 - Automatic kPa to bar conversion
 65	 - Color zones: Green (0.9-1.8bar), Yellow (1.8-2.2bar), Red (2.2bar+)
 66	
 67	3. **OBD2 Communication**
 68	 - Bluetooth connection to ELM327 dongle
 69	 - PID 0105: Engine Coolant Temperature
 70	 - PID 010B: Intake Manifold Absolute Pressure
 71	 - 2-second update interval
 72	
 73	### Display Features
 74	
 75	- **Analog Gauges**: Realistic needle movement with smooth animation
> 76	- **Color-coded Zones**: Visual indication of safe/warning/critical ranges
 77	- **Real-time Values**: Numeric display of current sensor readings
 78	- **Status Messages**: Connection status and error notifications
 79	- **Auto-reconnect**: Automatic Bluetooth reconnection on disconnect
 80	
 81	## Installation Instructions
...
 223	### Vehicle Compatibility
 224	
 225	**Supported Vehicles**: Any vehicle with OBD2 port (1996+ in US, 2001+ in EU)
 226	
 227	**Required OBD2 PIDs**:
> 228	- **PID 0105**: Engine Coolant Temperature (mandatory on most vehicles)
 229	- **PID 010B**: Intake Manifold Absolute Pressure (turbocharged vehicles)
 230	
 231	**Tested Vehicles**:
 232	- Turbocharged gasoline engines
 233	- Diesel engines with turbocharger

 1	/**
 2	 * @file SensorData.h
 3	 * @brief Data structure for automotive sensor readings
 4	 * @author Andreas Auer
 5	 */
 6	
 7	#ifndef SENSOR_DATA_H
 8	#define SENSOR_DATA_H
 9	
 10	#include <Arduino.h>
 11	
 12	/**
 13	 * @brief Structure to hold automotive sensor data
 14	 */
 15	struct SensorData {
 16	 float coolantTemperature; ///< Engine coolant temperature in Celsius
 17	 float boostPressure; ///< Intake manifold pressure in kPa
 18	 bool isValid; ///< Flag indicating if data is valid
 19	 unsigned long timestamp; ///< Timestamp of last update
 20	
 21	 /**
 22	 * @brief Default constructor
 23	 */
 24	 SensorData() : coolantTemperature(0.0f), boostPressure(0.0f), isValid(false), timestamp(0) {}
 25	
 26	 /**
 27	 * @brief Constructor with values
 28	 * @param temp Coolant temperature in Celsius
 29	 * @param pressure Boost pressure in kPa
 30	 */
 31	 SensorData(float temp, float pressure) 
 32	 : coolantTemperature(temp), boostPressure(pressure), isValid(true), timestamp(millis()) {}
 33	
 34	 /**
 35	 * @brief Update sensor data
 36	 * @param temp New coolant temperature
 37	 * @param pressure New boost pressure
 38	 */
 39	 void update(float temp, float pressure) {
 40	 coolantTemperature = temp;
 41	 boostPressure = pressure;
 42	 isValid = true;
 43	 timestamp = millis();
 44	 }
 45	
 46	 /**
 47	 * @brief Mark data as invalid
 48	 */
 49	 void invalidate() {
 50	 isValid = false;
 51	 }
 52	};
 53	
 54	#endif // SENSOR_DATA_H

 1	/**
 2	 * @file OBD2Manager.h
 3	 * @brief OBD2 communication manager for ESP32 Bluetooth connectivity
 4	 * @author Andreas Auer
 5	 */
 6	
 7	#ifndef OBD2_MANAGER_H
 8	#define OBD2_MANAGER_H
 9	
 10	#include <Arduino.h>
 11	#include <BluetoothSerial.h>
 12	#include "SensorData.h"
 13	
 14	/**
 15	 * @brief OBD2 connection states
 16	 */
 17	enum class OBD2State {
 18	 DISCONNECTED, ///< No Bluetooth connection
 19	 CONNECTED, ///< Bluetooth connected but OBD2 not initialized
 20	 INITIALIZING, ///< Sending initialization commands
 21	 READY, ///< Ready to receive sensor data
 22	 ERROR ///< Error state
 23	};
 24	
 25	/**
 26	 * @brief Manages OBD2 communication via Bluetooth
 27	 */
 28	class OBD2Manager {
 29	public:
 30	 /**
 31	 * @brief Constructor
 32	 * @param deviceName Bluetooth device name for pairing
 33	 */
 34	 explicit OBD2Manager(const String& deviceName = "ESP32_OBD2_Display");
 35	
 36	 /**
 37	 * @brief Destructor
 38	 */
 39	 ~OBD2Manager() = default;
 40	
 41	 /**
 42	 * @brief Initialize Bluetooth and start advertising
 43	 * @return True if initialization successful
 44	 */
 45	 bool begin();
 46	
 47	 /**
 48	 * @brief Update connection status and handle initialization
 49	 * Should be called regularly in main loop
 50	 */
 51	 void update();
 52	
 53	 /**
 54	 * @brief Request sensor data from OBD2 interface
 55	 * @return SensorData structure with current readings
 56	 */
 57	 SensorData requestSensorData();
 58	
 59	 /**
 60	 * @brief Get current connection state
 61	 * @return Current OBD2State
 62	 */
 63	 OBD2State getState() const { return currentState; }
 64	
 65	 /**
 66	 * @brief Check if OBD2 is ready for data requests
 67	 * @return True if ready
 68	 */
 69	 bool isReady() const { return currentState == OBD2State::READY; }
 70	
 71	 /**
 72	 * @brief Check if Bluetooth client is connected
 73	 * @return True if connected
 74	 */
 75	 bool isConnected();
 76	
 77	 /**
 78	 * @brief Get status string for display
 79	 * @return Human-readable status string
 80	 */
 81	 String getStatusString() const;
 82	
 83	private:
 84	 BluetoothSerial serialBT; ///< Bluetooth Serial interface
 85	 String deviceName; ///< Bluetooth device name
 86	 OBD2State currentState; ///< Current connection state
 87	 unsigned long lastInitCommand; ///< Timestamp of last init command
 88	 int initStep; ///< Current initialization step
 89	 SensorData lastSensorData; ///< Last valid sensor reading
 90	
 91	 // OBD2 Communication
 92	 /**
 93	 * @brief Send OBD2 command
 94	 * @param command Command string to send
 95	 */
 96	 void sendCommand(const String& command);
 97	
 98	 /**
 99	 * @brief Read response from OBD2 interface
 100	 * @param timeout Timeout in milliseconds
 101	 * @return Response string
 102	 */
 103	 String readResponse(unsigned long timeout = 3000);
 104	
 105	 /**
 106	 * @brief Initialize OBD2 interface with AT commands
 107	 */
 108	 void initializeOBD2();
 109	
 110	 /**
 111	 * @brief Parse coolant temperature from OBD2 response
 112	 * @param response Raw OBD2 response string
 113	 * @return Temperature in Celsius, or -999 if parsing failed
 114	 */
 115	 float parseCoolantTemp(const String& response);
 116	
 117	 /**
 118	 * @brief Parse boost pressure from OBD2 response
 119	 * @param response Raw OBD2 response string
 120	 * @return Pressure in kPa, or -999 if parsing failed
 121	 */
 122	 float parseBoostPressure(const String& response);
 123	
 124	 /**
 125	 * @brief Check if response is valid OBD2 data
 126	 * @param response Response string to validate
 127	 * @return True if valid
 128	 */
 129	 bool isValidResponse(const String& response);
 130	
 131	 // Constants
 132	 static const unsigned long INIT_COMMAND_DELAY = 500; ///< Delay between init commands
 133	 static const unsigned long RESPONSE_TIMEOUT = 3000; ///< Response timeout in ms
 134	 static const int MAX_INIT_RETRIES = 3; ///< Maximum initialization retries
 135	};
 136	
 137	#endif // OBD2_MANAGER_H

 1	/**
 2	 * @file OBD2Manager.cpp
 3	 * @brief Implementation of OBD2 communication manager
 4	 * @author Andreas Auer
 5	 */
 6	
 7	#include "OBD2Manager.h"
 8	#include "config.h"
 9	
 10	OBD2Manager::OBD2Manager(const String& deviceName)
 11	 : deviceName(deviceName), currentState(OBD2State::DISCONNECTED),
 12	 lastInitCommand(0), initStep(0) {
 13	}
 14	
 15	bool OBD2Manager::begin() {
 16	 Serial.println("Initializing OBD2Manager...");
 17	
 18	 if (!serialBT.begin(deviceName)) {
 19	 Serial.println("Failed to initialize Bluetooth");
 20	 currentState = OBD2State::ERROR;
 21	 return false;
 22	 }
 23	
 24	 Serial.println("Bluetooth initialized. Device name: " + deviceName);
 25	 currentState = OBD2State::DISCONNECTED;
 26	 return true;
 27	}
 28	
 29	void OBD2Manager::update() {
 30	 switch (currentState) {
 31	 case OBD2State::DISCONNECTED:
 32	 if (isConnected()) {
 33	 Serial.println("Bluetooth client connected");
 34	 currentState = OBD2State::CONNECTED;
 35	 initStep = 0;
 36	 lastInitCommand = 0;
 37	 }
 38	 break;
 39	
 40	 case OBD2State::CONNECTED:
 41	 if (!isConnected()) {
 42	 Serial.println("Bluetooth client disconnected");
 43	 currentState = OBD2State::DISCONNECTED;
 44	 } else {
 45	 currentState = OBD2State::INITIALIZING;
 46	 initializeOBD2();
 47	 }
 48	 break;
 49	
 50	 case OBD2State::INITIALIZING:

 201	String OBD2Manager::readResponse(unsigned long timeout) {
 202	 String response = "";

 209	 if (response.length() > 0) {
 210	 break;
 211	 }
 212	 } else if (c != ' ') { // Remove spaces
 213	 response += c;
 214	 }
 215	 }
 216	 delay(10);
 217	 }
 218	
 219	 Serial.println("Received: " + response);
 220	 return response;
 221	}

 1	#include <Arduino.h>
 2	#include "AutomotiveDisplay.h"
 3	#include "config.h"
 4	
 5	// Check if Bluetooth is available
 6	#if !defined(CONFIG_BT_ENABLED) || !defined(CONFIG_BLUEDROID_ENABLED)
 7	#error Bluetooth is not enabled! Please run `make menuconfig` to and enable it
 8	#endif
 9	
 10	// Main application instance
 11	AutomotiveDisplay automotiveDisplay;
 12	
 13	void setup() {
 14	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("ESP32 OBD2 Automotive Display Starting...");
 15	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("Firmware: " FIRMWARE_VERSION);
 16	
 17	 // Initialize the main application
 18	 if (!automotiveDisplay.begin()) {
 19	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("Failed to initialize automotive display!");
 20	 while (true) {
 21	 delay(1000); // Halt on critical error
 22	 }
 23	 }
 24	
 25	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("Setup complete!");
 26	}
 27	
 28	void loop() {
 29	 // Main application update loop
 30	 automotiveDisplay.update();
 31	
 32	 // Small delay to prevent excessive CPU usage
 33	 delay(DISPLAY_UPDATE_INTERVAL);
 34	}
 35	
 36	// All functionality now handled by AutomotiveDisplay class

 1	/**
 2	 * @file config.h
 3	 * @brief System configuration and settings for ESP32 Automotive Display
 4	 * @author Andreas Auer
 5	 * @version 1.0.0
 6	 * @date 2025-01-28
 7	 */
 8	
 9	#ifndef CONFIG_H
 10	#define CONFIG_H
 11	
 12	// ============================================================================
 13	// PROJECT METADATA
 14	// ============================================================================
 15	#define PROJECT_NAME "ESP32 OBD2 Automotive Display"
 16	#define PROJECT_VERSION "1.0.0"
 17	#define PROJECT_AUTHOR "Andreas Auer"
 18	#define PROJECT_EMAIL "andreas.auer03@gmail.com"
 19	#define PROJECT_DESCRIPTION "Real-time automotive sensor display with analog gauges"
 20	#define BUILD_DATE __DATE__
 21	#define BUILD_TIME __TIME__
 22	#define FIRMWARE_VERSION "v1.0.0-" __DATE__
 23	
 24	// ============================================================================
 25	// HARDWARE CONFIGURATION
 26	// ============================================================================
 27	
 28	// --- ESP32 Board Configuration ---
 29	#define ESP32_BOARD_TYPE "ESP32 DevKit v1"
 30	#define CPU_FREQUENCY_MHZ 240
 31	#define FLASH_SIZE_MB 4
 32	#define PSRAM_ENABLED false
 33	
 34	// --- TFT Display Configuration ---
 35	// Note: TFT_DRIVER (ILI9341) is defined in platformio.ini build_flags
 36	#define TFT_WIDTH 240
 37	#define TFT_HEIGHT 320
 38	#define TFT_ROTATION 0 // 0=Portrait, 1=Landscape, 2=Portrait flipped, 3=Landscape flipped
 39	
 40	// TFT SPI Pin Configuration
 41	#define TFT_MISO_PIN 19
 42	#define TFT_MOSI_PIN 23
 43	#define TFT_SCLK_PIN 18
 44	#define TFT_CS_PIN 15
 45	#define TFT_DC_PIN 2
 46	#define TFT_RST_PIN 4
 47	#define TFT_BL_PIN (-1) // Backlight pin (-1 if not used)
 48	
 49	// TFT SPI Settings
 50	#define TFT_SPI_FREQUENCY 27000000 // 27 MHz
 51	#define TFT_SPI_READ_FREQUENCY 20000000 // 20 MHz for reading
 52	
 53	// --- Bluetooth Configuration ---
 54	#define BT_DEVICE_NAME "ESP32_OBD2_Display"
 55	#define BT_PIN_CODE "1234" // Optional PIN for pairing
 56	#define BT_ENABLE_SSP true // Secure Simple Pairing
 57	#define BT_DISCOVERABLE true // Make device discoverable
 58	
 59	// --- OBD2 Configuration ---
 60	#define OBD2_BAUD_RATE 38400 // Standard OBD2 baud rate
 61	#define OBD2_TIMEOUT_MS 3000 // Response timeout
 62	#define OBD2_RETRY_COUNT 3 // Number of retries for failed commands
 63	#define OBD2_INIT_DELAY_MS 500 // Delay between initialization commands
 64	
 65	// ============================================================================
 66	// SENSOR CONFIGURATION
 67	// ============================================================================
 68	
 69	// --- Temperature Sensor (PID 0105) ---
 70	#define TEMP_PID "0105"
 71	#define TEMP_GAUGE_MIN_VALUE 40.0f // Gauge minimum for needle positioning (°C)
 72	#define TEMP_MAX_VALUE 130.0f // Maximum temperature (°C)
 73	#define TEMP_BLUE_THRESHOLD 80.0f // Blue zone upper limit (°C) - cold engine
 74	#define TEMP_GREEN_THRESHOLD 100.0f // Green zone upper limit (°C) - normal operating
 75	#define TEMP_YELLOW_THRESHOLD 115.0f // Yellow zone upper limit (°C) - warning hot
 76	#define TEMP_CRITICAL_VALUE 115.0f // Critical temperature warning (°C) - overheating
 77	#define TEMP_UNIT "°C"
 78	#define TEMP_TITLE "Coolant"
 79	#define TEMP_NEEDLE_MIN_TEMP 40.0f // Temperature below which needle stays at 0%
 80	
 81	// --- Boost Pressure Sensor (PID 010B) ---
 82	#define BOOST_PID "010B"
 83	#define BOOST_MIN_VALUE 0.9f // Minimum pressure (bar)
 84	#define BOOST_MAX_VALUE 2.5f // Maximum pressure (bar)
 85	#define BOOST_GREEN_THRESHOLD 1.8f // Green zone upper limit (bar)
 86	#define BOOST_YELLOW_THRESHOLD 2.2f // Yellow zone upper limit (bar)
 87	#define BOOST_CRITICAL_VALUE 2.4f // Critical pressure warning (bar)
 88	#define BOOST_UNIT "bar"
 89	#define BOOST_TITLE "Boost"
 90	
 91	// ============================================================================
 92	// BATTERY VOLTAGE MONITORING CONFIGURATION
 93	// ============================================================================
 94	
 95	// --- Battery Voltage Settings ---
 96	#define BATTERY_VOLTAGE_ENABLED true // Enable battery voltage monitoring
 97	#define BATTERY_UPDATE_INTERVAL 3000 // Update interval in milliseconds
 98	#define VOLTAGE_LOW_THRESHOLD 12.5f // Critical low voltage (red zone)
 99	#define VOLTAGE_MID_THRESHOLD 13.0f // Warning/normal boundary (yellow/green)
 100	#define VOLTAGE_HIGH_THRESHOLD 14.5f // Upper normal range limit
 101	#define VOLTAGE_DISPLAY_DECIMALS 1 // Decimal places to display
 102	#define VOLTAGE_DISPLAY_X 200 // X position for voltage display
 103	#define VOLTAGE_DISPLAY_Y 10 // Y position for voltage display
 104	#define VOLTAGE_FONT_SIZE 2 // Font size (1-4)
 105	
 106	// --- Data Validation Ranges ---
 107	#define TEMP_VALID_MIN (-40.0f) // Absolute minimum valid temperature
 108	#define TEMP_VALID_MAX 150.0f // Absolute maximum valid temperature
 109	#define BOOST_VALID_MIN 0.0f // Absolute minimum valid pressure (bar)
 110	#define BOOST_VALID_MAX 3.0f // Absolute maximum valid pressure (bar)
 111	
 112	// ============================================================================
 113	// DISPLAY CONFIGURATION
 114	// ============================================================================
 115	
 116	// --- Screen Layout ---
 117	#define SCREEN_WIDTH 240
 118	#define SCREEN_HEIGHT 320
 119	#define STATUS_BAR_HEIGHT 30
 120	#define GAUGE_AREA_HEIGHT (SCREEN_HEIGHT - STATUS_BAR_HEIGHT)
 121	
 122	// --- Gauge Configuration ---
 123	#define GAUGE_RADIUS 80
 124	#define GAUGE_NEEDLE_LENGTH 65 // Length of gauge needle
 125	#define GAUGE_TICK_COUNT 8 // Number of major tick marks
 126	#define GAUGE_MINOR_TICKS true // Enable minor tick marks
 127	#define GAUGE_CENTER_DOT_SIZE 3 // Radius of center dot
 128	
 129	// Gauge Positions
 130	#define TEMP_GAUGE_X (SCREEN_WIDTH / 2)
 131	#define TEMP_GAUGE_Y 100
 132	#define BOOST_GAUGE_X (SCREEN_WIDTH / 2)
 133	#define BOOST_GAUGE_Y 220
 134	
 135	// --- Color Scheme ---
 136	#define COLOR_BACKGROUND 0x0000 // Black
 137	#define COLOR_TEXT 0xFFFF // White
 138	#define COLOR_GAUGE_OUTLINE 0xFFFF // White
 139	#define COLOR_NEEDLE 0xF800 // Red
 140	#define COLOR_BLUE_ZONE 0x001F // Blue (cold engine)
 141	#define COLOR_GREEN_ZONE 0x07E0 // Green (normal operating)
 142	#define COLOR_YELLOW_ZONE 0xFFE0 // Yellow (warning hot)
 143	#define COLOR_RED_ZONE 0xF800 // Red (overheating)
 144	
 145	// --- Battery Voltage Status Colors ---
 146	#define COLOR_STATUS_OK 0x07E0 // Green (normal voltage)
 147	#define COLOR_STATUS_WARNING 0xFFE0 // Yellow (low voltage warning)
 148	#define COLOR_STATUS_ERROR 0xF800 // Red (critical voltage)
 149	#define COLOR_TICK_MARKS 0xC618 // Light Grey
 150	#define COLOR_STATUS_OK 0x07E0 // Green
 151	#define COLOR_STATUS_WARNING 0xFFE0 // Yellow
 152	#define COLOR_STATUS_ERROR 0xF800 // Red
 153	
 154	// --- Text Configuration ---
 155	#define FONT_SIZE_SMALL 1
 156	#define FONT_SIZE_MEDIUM 2
 157	#define FONT_SIZE_LARGE 3
 158	#define TEXT_PADDING 5
 159	
 160	// ============================================================================
 161	// TIMING CONFIGURATION
 162	// ============================================================================
 163	
 164	// --- Update Intervals ---
 165	#define SENSOR_UPDATE_INTERVAL 2000 // Sensor data update interval (ms)
 166	#define DISPLAY_UPDATE_INTERVAL 100 // Display refresh interval (ms)
 167	#define STATUS_UPDATE_INTERVAL 1000 // Status check interval (ms)
 168	#define HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL 5000 // Heartbeat/keepalive interval (ms)
 169	
 170	// --- Timeout Values ---
 171	#define BT_CONNECTION_TIMEOUT 30000 // Bluetooth connection timeout (ms)
 172	#define OBD2_INIT_TIMEOUT 10000 // OBD2 initialization timeout (ms)
 173	#define SENSOR_DATA_TIMEOUT 5000 // Sensor data validity timeout (ms)
 174	#define ERROR_RETRY_INTERVAL 5000 // Error recovery retry interval (ms)
 175	
 176	// ============================================================================
 177	// SYSTEM BEHAVIOR
 178	// ============================================================================
 179	
 180	// --- Debug Configuration (Production) ---
 181	#define DEBUG_ENABLED false // Disable debug output for production
 182	#define DEBUG_SERIAL_BAUD 115200 // Serial debug baud rate (keep for emergency)
 183	#define DEBUG_OBD2_COMMANDS false // Disable OBD2 command logging
 184	#define DEBUG_SENSOR_DATA false // Disable sensor data logging
 185	#define DEBUG_DISPLAY_UPDATES false // Disable display update logging
 186	
 187	// --- Error Handling ---
 188	#define AUTO_RETRY_ENABLED true // Automatically retry failed operations
 189	#define MAX_CONSECUTIVE_ERRORS 5 // Max errors before entering error state
 190	#define WATCHDOG_ENABLED true // Enable software watchdog
 191	#define WATCHDOG_TIMEOUT_MS 30000 // Watchdog timeout
 192	
 193	// --- Power Management ---
 194	#define SLEEP_MODE_ENABLED false // Enable sleep mode when idle
 195	#define SLEEP_TIMEOUT_MS 300000 // Sleep after 5 minutes of inactivity
 196	#define WAKE_ON_BT_ENABLED true // Wake up on Bluetooth activity
 197	
 198	// ============================================================================
 199	// PRODUCTION HARDWARE FLAGS
 200	// ============================================================================

 14	bool AutomotiveDisplay::begin() {
 15	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("Initializing AutomotiveDisplay...");
 16	 
 17	 // Initialize Serial for debugging
 18	 Serial.begin(DEBUG_SERIAL_BAUD);
 19	 delay(1000);
 20	 
 21	 DEBUG_PRINTLN(PROJECT_NAME " " PROJECT_VERSION);
 22	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("Build: " BUILD_DATE " " BUILD_TIME);
 23	 
 24	 // Initialize display first
 25	 if (!gaugeDisplay.begin()) {
 26	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("Failed to initialize display");
 27	 currentState = AppState::ERROR;
 28	 return false;
 29	 }
 30	 
 31	 // Initialize OBD2 manager
 32	 if (!obd2Manager.begin()) {
 33	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("Failed to initialize OBD2 manager");
 34	 currentState = AppState::ERROR;
 35	 return false;
 36	 }
 37	 
 38	 // Show initial status
 39	 gaugeDisplay.showStatus("System Ready");
 40	 
 41	 currentState = AppState::WAITING_BT;
 42	 lastUpdate = millis();
 43	 lastStateCheck = millis();
 44	 
 45	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("AutomotiveDisplay initialization complete");
 46	 return true;
 47	}
 48	
 49	void AutomotiveDisplay::update() {
 50	 unsigned long currentTime = millis();
 51	 
 52	 // Update OBD2 manager state
 53	 obd2Manager.update();
 54	 
 55	 // Handle state transitions
 56	 if (currentTime - lastStateCheck >= STATE_CHECK_INTERVAL) {
 57	 handleStateTransitions();
 58	 lastStateCheck = currentTime;
 59	 }
 60	 
 61	 // Update sensor data and display
 62	 if (currentTime - lastUpdate >= UPDATE_INTERVAL) {
 63	 switch (currentState) {
 64	 case AppState::RUNNING:
 65	 updateSensorData();
 66	 updateDisplay();
 67	 break;
 68	 
 69	 case AppState::WAITING_BT:
 70	 gaugeDisplay.showStatus("Waiting for Bluetooth...");
 71	 break;
 72	 
 73	 case AppState::CONNECTING_OBD2:
 74	 gaugeDisplay.showStatus("Connecting to OBD2...");
 75	 break;
 76	 
 77	 case AppState::ERROR:
 78	 handleErrors();
 79	 break;
 80	 
 81	 default:
 82	 break;
 83	 }
 84	 
 85	 lastUpdate = currentTime;
 86	 }
 87	 
 88	 // Log debug info periodically
 89	 if (DEBUG_ENABLED && (currentTime % HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL == 0)) {
 90	 logDebugInfo();
 91	 }
 92	}

 94	String AutomotiveDisplay::getStatusString() const {
 95	 switch (currentState) {
 96	 case AppState::INITIALIZING: return "Initializing";
 97	 case AppState::WAITING_BT: return "Waiting for Bluetooth";
 98	 case AppState::CONNECTING_OBD2: return "Connecting to OBD2";
 99	 case AppState::RUNNING: return "Running";
 100	 case AppState::ERROR: return "Error";
 101	 default: return "Unknown";
 102	 }
 103	}
 104	
 105	void AutomotiveDisplay::handleStateTransitions() {
 106	 switch (currentState) {
 107	 case AppState::WAITING_BT:
 108	 if (obd2Manager.isConnected()) {
 109	 currentState = AppState::CONNECTING_OBD2;
 110	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("State: WAITING_BT -> CONNECTING_OBD2");
 111	 }
 112	 break;
 113	 
 114	 case AppState::CONNECTING_OBD2:
 115	 if (!obd2Manager.isConnected()) {
 116	 currentState = AppState::WAITING_BT;
 117	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("State: CONNECTING_OBD2 -> WAITING_BT");
 118	 } else if (obd2Manager.isReady()) {
 119	 currentState = AppState::RUNNING;
 120	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("State: CONNECTING_OBD2 -> RUNNING");
 121	 gaugeDisplay.showStatus("OBD2 Connected");
 122	 }
 123	 break;
 124	 
 125	 case AppState::RUNNING:
 126	 if (!obd2Manager.isConnected()) {
 127	 currentState = AppState::WAITING_BT;
 128	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("State: RUNNING -> WAITING_BT");
 129	 currentSensorData.invalidate();
 130	 } else if (!obd2Manager.isReady()) {
 131	 currentState = AppState::CONNECTING_OBD2;
 132	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("State: RUNNING -> CONNECTING_OBD2");
 133	 }
 134	 break;
 135	 
 136	 case AppState::ERROR:
 137	 // Try to recover after delay
 138	 if (millis() - lastStateCheck > ERROR_RETRY_INTERVAL) {
 139	 currentState = AppState::INITIALIZING;
 140	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("State: ERROR -> INITIALIZING (retry)");
 141	 begin(); // Reinitialize
 142	 }
 143	 break;
 144	 
 145	 default:
 146	 break;
 147	 }
 148	}

 150	void AutomotiveDisplay::updateSensorData() {
 151	 if (obd2Manager.isReady()) {
 152	 const SensorData newData = obd2Manager.requestSensorData();
 153	 if (newData.isValid) {
 154	 currentSensorData = newData;
 155	 DEBUG_PRINTF("Sensor data updated: Temp=%.1f°C, Boost=%.2fbar\n", 
 156	 newData.coolantTemperature, newData.boostPressure);
 157	 }
 158	 }
 159	}

 161	void AutomotiveDisplay::updateDisplay() {
 162	 if (currentSensorData.isValid) {
 163	 gaugeDisplay.updateGauges(currentSensorData);
 164	 
 165	 // Check for critical values and update status
 166	 String status = "Normal";
 167	 if (currentSensorData.coolantTemperature > TEMP_CRITICAL_VALUE) {
 168	 status = "HIGH TEMP WARNING!";
 169	 } else if (currentSensorData.boostPressure > BOOST_CRITICAL_VALUE) {
 170	 status = "HIGH BOOST WARNING!";
 171	 }
 172	 #if BATTERY_VOLTAGE_ENABLED
 173	 else if (currentSensorData.batteryVoltageValid) {
 174	 if (currentSensorData.batteryVoltage < VOLTAGE_LOW_THRESHOLD) {
 175	 status = "LOW BATTERY WARNING!";
 176	 } else if (currentSensorData.batteryVoltage > VOLTAGE_HIGH_THRESHOLD) {
 177	 status = "HIGH VOLTAGE WARNING!";
 178	 }
 179	 }
 180	 #endif
 181	
 182	 if (status != "Normal") {
 183	 gaugeDisplay.showStatus(status);
 184	 }

 12	OBD2Manager::OBD2Manager(String deviceName)
 13	 : deviceName(std::move(deviceName)), currentState(OBD2State::DISCONNECTED),
 14	 lastInitCommand(0), initStep(0), lastBatteryUpdate(0) {
 15	}
 16	
 17	bool OBD2Manager::begin() {
 18	 Serial.println("Initializing OBD2Manager...");
 19	
 20	 if (!serialBT.begin(deviceName)) {
 21	 Serial.println("Failed to initialize Bluetooth");
 22	 currentState = OBD2State::ERROR;
 23	 return false;
 24	 }
 25	
 26	 Serial.println("Bluetooth initialized. Device name: " + deviceName);
 27	 currentState = OBD2State::DISCONNECTED;
 28	 return true;
 29	}

 31	void OBD2Manager::update() {
 32	 switch (currentState) {
 33	 case OBD2State::DISCONNECTED:
 34	 if (isConnected()) {
 35	 Serial.println("Bluetooth client connected");
 36	 currentState = OBD2State::CONNECTED;
 37	 initStep = 0;
 38	 lastInitCommand = 0;
 39	 }
 40	 break;
 41	
 42	 case OBD2State::CONNECTED:
 43	 if (!isConnected()) {
 44	 Serial.println("Bluetooth client disconnected");
 45	 currentState = OBD2State::DISCONNECTED;
 46	 } else {
 47	 currentState = OBD2State::INITIALIZING;
 48	 initializeOBD2();
 49	 }
 50	 break;
 51	
 52	 case OBD2State::INITIALIZING:
 53	 if (!isConnected()) {
 54	 currentState = OBD2State::DISCONNECTED;
 55	 }
 56	 // Initialization is handled in initializeOBD2()
 57	 break;
 58	
 59	 case OBD2State::READY:
 60	 if (!isConnected()) {
 61	 Serial.println("Connection lost during operation");
 62	 currentState = OBD2State::DISCONNECTED;
 63	 }
 64	 break;

 158	void OBD2Manager::initializeOBD2() {
 159	 if (millis() - lastInitCommand < INIT_COMMAND_DELAY) {
 160	 return; // Wait for delay between commands
 161	 }
 162	
 163	 if (initStep < 6) {
 164	 const String descriptions[] = {"Reset", "Echo off", "Linefeeds off", "Spaces off", "Headers off", "Auto protocol"};
 165	 const String commands[] = {"ATZ", "ATE0", "ATL0", "ATS0", "ATH0", "ATSP0"};
 166	 Serial.println("Init step " + String(initStep) + ": " + descriptions[initStep]);
 167	 sendCommand(commands[initStep]);
 168	 lastInitCommand = millis();
 169	 initStep++;
 170	 } else {
 171	 Serial.println("OBD2 initialization complete");
 172	 currentState = OBD2State::READY;
 173	 }
 174	}

 224	String OBD2Manager::readResponse(const unsigned long timeout) {
 225	 String response = "";
 226	 const unsigned long startTime = millis();
 227	
 228	 while (millis() - startTime < timeout) {
 229	 if (serialBT.available()) {
 230	 const char c = serialBT.read();
 231	 if (c == '\r' || c == '\n') {
 232	 if (response.length() > 0) {
 233	 break;
 234	 }
 235	 } else if (c != ' ') { // Remove spaces
 236	 response += c;
 237	 }
 238	 }
 239	 delay(10);
 240	 }
 241	
 242	 Serial.println("Received: " + response);
 243	 return response;
 244	}

 70	void GaugeDisplay::drawAnalogGauge(const GaugeConfig& config, float value) {
 71	 // Draw color zones first (background)
 72	 drawColorZones(config);
 73	
 74	 // Draw gauge outline
 75	 tft.drawCircle(config.centerX, config.centerY, config.radius, COLOR_GAUGE_OUTLINE);
 76	 tft.drawCircle(config.centerX, config.centerY, config.radius - 1, COLOR_GAUGE_OUTLINE);
 77	
 78	 // Draw tick marks and labels
 79	 drawGaugeTicks(config, 8);
 80	
 81	 // Draw title
 82	 tft.setTextSize(1);
 83	 tft.setTextColor(COLOR_TEXT);
 84	 const int titleWidth = config.title.length() * 6;
 85	 tft.drawString(config.title, config.centerX - titleWidth/2, config.centerY - config.radius - 20);
 86	
 87	 // Draw current value (always show actual value)
 88	 const String valueStr = String(value, 1) + config.unit;
 89	 tft.setTextSize(2);
 90	 const int valueWidth = valueStr.length() * 12;
 91	 tft.drawString(valueStr, config.centerX - valueWidth/2, config.centerY + config.radius + 10);
 92	
 93	 // Calculate needle position value
 94	 float needleValue = value;
 95	
 96	 // Special handling for temperature gauge: needle stays at minimum if temp < 40°C
 97	 if (config.title == "Coolant" && value < TEMP_NEEDLE_MIN_TEMP) {
 98	 needleValue = config.minValue; // Use gauge minimum for needle position
 99	 }
 100	
 101	 // Draw needle
 102	 drawNeedle(config, needleValue);
 103	
 104	 // Draw center dot
 105	 tft.fillCircle(config.centerX, config.centerY, 3, config.needleColor);
 106	}
 107	
 108	void GaugeDisplay::drawGaugeTicks(const GaugeConfig& config, const int numTicks) {
 109	 for (int i = 0; i <= numTicks; i++) {
 110	 const float angle = mapFloat(i, 0, numTicks, -90.0, 90.0);
 111	 const float angleRad = (angle + 90) * PI / 180.0;
 112	
 113	 const int outerX = config.centerX + (config.radius - 5) * cos(angleRad);
 114	 const int outerY = config.centerY + (config.radius - 5) * sin(angleRad);
 115	 const int innerX = config.centerX + (config.radius - 15) * cos(angleRad);
 116	 const int innerY = config.centerY + (config.radius - 15) * sin(angleRad);
 117	
 118	 tft.drawLine(outerX, outerY, innerX, innerY, COLOR_TICK);
 119	
 120	 // Draw tick labels
 121	 if (i % 2 == 0) { // Only label every other tick
 122	 const float tickValue = mapFloat(i, 0, numTicks, config.minValue, config.maxValue);
 123	 auto tickLabel = String(static_cast<int>(tickValue));
 124	
 125	 const int labelX = config.centerX + (config.radius - 25) * cos(angleRad);
 126	 const int labelY = config.centerY + (config.radius - 25) * sin(angleRad);
 127	
 128	 tft.setTextSize(1);
 129	 tft.setTextColor(COLOR_TEXT);
 130	 const int labelWidth = tickLabel.length() * 6;
 131	 tft.drawString(tickLabel, labelX - labelWidth/2, labelY - 4);
 132	 }
 133	 }
 134	}

 163	void GaugeDisplay::drawColorZones(const GaugeConfig& config) {
 164	 // Draw colored arcs for different zones
 165	 const int numSegments = 60;
 166	
 167	 for (int i = 0; i < numSegments; i++) {
 168	 const float angle1 = mapFloat(i, 0, numSegments, -90.0, 90.0);
 169	 const float angle2 = mapFloat(i + 1, 0, numSegments, -90.0, 90.0);
 170	
 171	 const float value = mapFloat(i, 0, numSegments, config.minValue, config.maxValue);
 172	
 173	 uint16_t color;
 174	 if (value <= config.blueThreshold) {
 175	 color = COLOR_BLUE_ZONE; // Cold engine (below 80°C)
 176	 } else if (value <= config.greenThreshold) {
 177	 color = COLOR_GREEN_ZONE; // Normal operating (80-100°C)
 178	 } else if (value <= config.yellowThreshold) {
 179	 color = COLOR_YELLOW_ZONE; // Warning hot (100-115°C)
 180	 } else {
 181	 color = COLOR_RED_ZONE; // Overheating (115°C+)
 182	 }

 161	void AutomotiveDisplay::updateDisplay() {
 162	 if (currentSensorData.isValid) {
 163	 gaugeDisplay.updateGauges(currentSensorData);
 164	 
 165	 // Check for critical values and update status
 166	 String status = "Normal";
 167	 if (currentSensorData.coolantTemperature > TEMP_CRITICAL_VALUE) {
 168	 status = "HIGH TEMP WARNING!";
 169	 } else if (currentSensorData.boostPressure > BOOST_CRITICAL_VALUE) {
 170	 status = "HIGH BOOST WARNING!";
 171	 }
 172	 #if BATTERY_VOLTAGE_ENABLED
 173	 else if (currentSensorData.batteryVoltageValid) {
 174	 if (currentSensorData.batteryVoltage < VOLTAGE_LOW_THRESHOLD) {
 175	 status = "LOW BATTERY WARNING!";
 176	 } else if (currentSensorData.batteryVoltage > VOLTAGE_HIGH_THRESHOLD) {
 177	 status = "HIGH VOLTAGE WARNING!";
 178	 }
 179	 }
 180	 #endif
 181	
 182	 if (status != "Normal") {
 183	 gaugeDisplay.showStatus(status);
 184	 }

 2	 * @file SensorData.h
 3	 * @brief Data structure for automotive sensor readings

 5	 */
 6	
 7	#ifndef SENSOR_DATA_H
 8	#define SENSOR_DATA_H
 9	
 10	#include <Arduino.h>
 11	
 12	/**
 13	 * @brief Structure to hold automotive sensor data
 14	 */
 15	struct SensorData {
 16	 // Existing sensor fields (preserve exactly)
 17	 float coolantTemperature; ///< Engine coolant temperature in Celsius
 18	 float boostPressure; ///< Intake manifold pressure in kPa
 19	 bool isValid; ///< Flag indicating if data is valid
 20	 unsigned long timestamp; ///< Timestamp of last update
 21	
 22	 // New battery voltage fields
 23	 float batteryVoltage; ///< Battery voltage in volts
 24	 bool batteryVoltageValid; ///< True if voltage reading is valid
 25	 unsigned long batteryTimestamp; ///< Timestamp of last voltage reading
 26	
 27	 /**
 28	 * @brief Default constructor
 29	 */
 30	 SensorData() : coolantTemperature(0.0f), boostPressure(0.0f), isValid(false), timestamp(0),
 31	 batteryVoltage(0.0f), batteryVoltageValid(false), batteryTimestamp(0) {}
 32	
 33	 /**
 34	 * @brief Constructor with values
 35	 * @param temp Coolant temperature in Celsius
 36	 * @param pressure Boost pressure in kPa
 37	 */
 38	 SensorData(float temp, float pressure)
 39	 : coolantTemperature(temp), boostPressure(pressure), isValid(true), timestamp(millis()),
 40	 batteryVoltage(0.0f), batteryVoltageValid(false), batteryTimestamp(0) {}
 41	
 42	 /**
 43	 * @brief Update sensor data
 44	 * @param temp New coolant temperature
 45	 * @param pressure New boost pressure
 46	 */
 47	 void update(float temp, float pressure) {
 48	 coolantTemperature = temp;
 49	 boostPressure = pressure;
 50	 isValid = true;
 51	 timestamp = millis();
 52	 }
 53	
 54	 /**
 55	 * @brief Update battery voltage
 56	 * @param voltage New battery voltage in volts
 57	 */
 58	 void updateBatteryVoltage(float voltage) {
 59	 batteryVoltage = voltage;
 60	 batteryVoltageValid = true;
 61	 batteryTimestamp = millis();
 62	 }
 63	
 64	 /**
 65	 * @brief Mark battery voltage as invalid
 66	 */
 67	 void invalidateBatteryVoltage() {
 68	 batteryVoltageValid = false;
 69	 }
 70	
 71	 /**
 72	 * @brief Mark data as invalid
 73	 */
 74	 void invalidate() {
 75	 isValid = false;
 76	 }
 77	};
 78	
 79	#endif // SENSOR_DATA_H

 1	/**
 2	 * @file AutomotiveDisplay.h
 3	 * @brief Main application class for ESP32 automotive display
 4	 * @author Andreas Auer
 5	 */
 6	
 7	#ifndef AUTOMOTIVE_DISPLAY_H
 8	#define AUTOMOTIVE_DISPLAY_H
 9	
 10	#include <Arduino.h>
 11	#include "OBD2Manager.h"
 12	#include "GaugeDisplay.h"
 13	#include "SensorData.h"
 14	
 15	/**
 16	 * @brief Application states
 17	 */
 18	enum class AppState {
 19	 INITIALIZING, ///< System initialization
 20	 WAITING_BT, ///< Waiting for Bluetooth connection
 21	 CONNECTING_OBD2, ///< Connecting to OBD2 interface
 22	 RUNNING, ///< Normal operation
 23	 ERROR ///< Error state
 24	};
 25	
 26	/**
 27	 * @brief Main application class coordinating all components
 28	 */
 29	class AutomotiveDisplay {
 30	public:
 31	 /**
 32	 * @brief Constructor
 33	 */
 34	 AutomotiveDisplay();
 35	
 36	 /**
 37	 * @brief Destructor
 38	 */
 39	 ~AutomotiveDisplay() = default;
 40	
 41	 /**
 42	 * @brief Initialize all components
 43	 * @return True if initialization successful
 44	 */
 45	 bool begin();
 46	
 47	 /**
 48	 * @brief Main update loop - call this in Arduino loop()
 49	 */
 50	 void update();
 51	
 52	 /**
 53	 * @brief Get current application state
 54	 * @return Current AppState
 55	 */
 56	 AppState getState() const { return currentState; }
 57	
 58	 /**
 59	 * @brief Get status string for debugging
 60	 * @return Human-readable status string
 61	 */
 62	 String getStatusString() const;
 63	
 64	private:
 65	 OBD2Manager obd2Manager; ///< OBD2 communication manager
 66	 GaugeDisplay gaugeDisplay; ///< Display manager
 67	 AppState currentState; ///< Current application state
 68	 SensorData currentSensorData; ///< Current sensor readings
 69	 
 70	 // Timing variables
 71	 unsigned long lastUpdate; ///< Last sensor update timestamp
 72	 unsigned long lastStateCheck; ///< Last state check timestamp
 73	 
 74	 /**
 75	 * @brief Handle state transitions
 76	 */
 77	 void handleStateTransitions();
 78	
 79	 /**
 80	 * @brief Update sensor data from OBD2
 81	 */
 82	 void updateSensorData();
 83	
 84	 /**
 85	 * @brief Update display with current data
 86	 */
 87	 void updateDisplay();
 88	
 89	 /**
 90	 * @brief Handle error conditions
 91	 */
 92	 void handleErrors();
 93	
 94	 /**
 95	 * @brief Log debug information to Serial
 96	 */
 97	 void logDebugInfo();
 98	
 99	 // Configuration constants
 100	 static constexpr unsigned long UPDATE_INTERVAL = 2000; ///< Sensor update interval (ms)
 101	 static constexpr unsigned long STATE_CHECK_INTERVAL = 1000; ///< State check interval (ms)
 102	 static constexpr unsigned long ERROR_RETRY_INTERVAL = 5000; ///< Error retry interval (ms)
 103	};
 104	
 105	#endif // AUTOMOTIVE_DISPLAY_H

 40

 42	 lastUpdate = millis();
 43	 lastStateCheck = millis();
 44	 
 45	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("AutomotiveDisplay initialization complete");
 46	 return true;
 47	}

 69	void AutomotiveDisplay::update() {
 70	 unsigned long currentTime = millis();
 71	 
 72	 // Update OBD2 manager state
 73	 obd2Manager.update();
 74	 
 75	 // Handle state transitions

 77	 handleStateTransitions();
 78	 lastStateCheck = currentTime;
 79	 }
 80	 
 81	 // Update sensor data and display

 84	 case AppState::RUNNING:
 85	 updateSensorData();
 86	 updateDisplay();
 87	 break;
 88	 
 89	 case AppState::WAITING_BT:
 90	 gaugeDisplay.showStatus("Waiting for Bluetooth...");
 91	 break;
 92	 
 93	 case AppState::CONNECTING_OBD2:
 94	 gaugeDisplay.showStatus("Connecting to OBD2...");
 95	 break;
 96	 
 97	 case AppState::ERROR:
 98	 handleErrors();
 99	 break;

","workspace_file_chunks":[]},{"status":"success","request_id":"c119e703-f22f-4dd7-8af8-bae97eec1fd2","request_message":"","structured_output_nodes":[{"id":1,"type":5,"content":"","tool_use":{"tool_use_id":"toolu_vrtx_01XXxCJPvSbNxMGirN9NfjJ8","tool_name":"str-replace-editor","input_json":"{"command": "str_replace", "path": "src/AutomotiveDisplay.cpp", "instruction_reminder": "ALWAYS BREAK DOWN EDITS INTO SMALLER CHUNKS OF AT MOST 150 LINES EACH.", "old_str": " #endif\n}", "old_str_start_line_number": 242, "old_str_end_line_number": 243, "new_str": " #endif\n}\n\n#ifdef DISPLAY_TEST\nvoid AutomotiveDisplay::updateDisplayTest() {\n unsigned long currentTime = millis();\n \n // Update test data generation\n generateTestData();\n \n // Update display with test data\n updateDisplay();\n \n // Update status message periodically\n if (currentTime - lastStatusUpdate >= TEST_STATUS_INTERVAL) {\n String statusMsg = TEST_CONNECTION_MSG;\n statusMsg += \" - T:\" + String(testTemperature, 1) + \"\u00b0C\";\n statusMsg += \" P:\" + String(testPressure, 1) + \"bar\";\n statusMsg += \" V:\" + String(testVoltage, 1) + \"V\";\n gaugeDisplay.showStatus(statusMsg);\n lastStatusUpdate = currentTime;\n \n #if TEST_ENABLE_DEBUG\n DEBUG_PRINTF(\"Display Test - Temp: %.1f\u00b0C, Pressure: %.1fbar, Voltage: %.1fV\\n\",\n testTemperature, testPressure, testVoltage);\n #endif\n }\n}\n\nvoid AutomotiveDisplay::generateTestData() {\n unsigned long currentTime = millis();\n \n // Update temperature\n if (currentTime - lastTempUpdate >= TEST_TEMP_INTERVAL) {\n if (testTempIncreasing) {\n testTemperature += TEST_TEMP_STEP;\n if (testTemperature >= TEST_TEMP_MAX) {\n testTemperature = TEST_TEMP_MIN; // Reset to minimum\n }\n }\n \n // Update sensor data with test temperature\n currentSensorData.coolantTemperature = testTemperature;\n currentSensorData.isValid = true;\n currentSensorData.timestamp = currentTime;\n \n lastTempUpdate = currentTime;\n }\n \n // Update pressure\n if (currentTime - lastPressureUpdate >= TEST_PRESSURE_INTERVAL) {\n if (testPressureIncreasing) {\n testPressure += TEST_PRESSURE_STEP;\n if (testPressure >= TEST_PRESSURE_MAX) {\n testPressure = TEST_PRESSURE_MIN; // Reset to minimum\n testPressureIncreasing = true;\n }\n }\n \n // Update sensor data with test pressure\n currentSensorData.boostPressure = testPressure;\n \n lastPressureUpdate = currentTime;\n }\n \n // Update voltage\n if (currentTime - lastVoltageUpdate >= TEST_VOLTAGE_INTERVAL) {\n if (testVoltageIncreasing) {\n testVoltage += TEST_VOLTAGE_STEP;\n if (testVoltage >= TEST_VOLTAGE_MAX) {\n testVoltage = TEST_VOLTAGE_MIN; // Reset to minimum\n }\n }\n \n // Update sensor data with test voltage\n #if BATTERY_VOLTAGE_ENABLED\n currentSensorData.updateBatteryVoltage(testVoltage);\n #endif\n \n lastVoltageUpdate = currentTime;\n }\n}\n#endif"}","mcp_server_name":"","mcp_tool_name":""}},{"id":2,"type":0,"content":""},{"id":3,"type":2,"content":""},{"id":4,"type":3,"content":""}],"seen_state":"seen","structured_request_nodes":[{"id":1,"type":1,"tool_result_node":{"content":"Here's the result of running `cat -n` on src/AutomotiveDisplay.cpp:

## Display Test Mode Implementation - Summary

I have successfully implemented a `DISPLAY_TEST` define that disables Bluetooth functionality and tests the display with simulated gauge data. Here's what was added:

### ✅ **Files Modified:**

1. **`include/config.h`** - Added display test configuration section
2. **`src/main.cpp`** - Modified to conditionally disable Bluetooth checks and show test mode info
3. **`include/AutomotiveDisplay.h`** - Added display test method declarations and state variables
4. **`src/AutomotiveDisplay.cpp`** - Implemented display test functionality

### ✅ **Key Features Implemented:**

**Display Test Configuration (in `config.h`):**
<augment_code_snippet path="include/config.h" mode="EXCERPT">
```cpp
// --- Display Test Mode ---
// Uncomment the following line to enable display test mode
// This disables Bluetooth functionality and simulates sensor data for display testing
// #define DISPLAY_TEST

#ifdef DISPLAY_TEST
 // --- Temperature Test Parameters ---
 #define TEST_TEMP_MIN -20.0f // Starting temperature (°C)
 #define TEST_TEMP_MAX 135.0f // Maximum temperature (°C)
 #define TEST_TEMP_STEP 5.0f // Temperature increment per step (°C)
 #define TEST_TEMP_INTERVAL 1000 // Update interval in milliseconds

 // --- Turbo Pressure Test Parameters ---
 #define TEST_PRESSURE_MIN 0.9f // Minimum pressure (bar)
 #define TEST_PRESSURE_MAX 2.5f // Maximum pressure (bar)
 #define TEST_PRESSURE_STEP 0.1f // Pressure increment per step (bar)
 #define TEST_PRESSURE_INTERVAL 1500 // Update interval in milliseconds

 // --- Battery Voltage Test Parameters ---
 #define TEST_VOLTAGE_MIN 11.5f // Starting voltage (V)
 #define TEST_VOLTAGE_MAX 16.0f // Maximum voltage (V)
 #define TEST_VOLTAGE_STEP 0.25f // Voltage increment per step (V)
 #define TEST_VOLTAGE_INTERVAL 2000 // Update interval in milliseconds
#endif
```
</augment_code_snippet>

**Simulation Behavior:**
- **Temperature Gauge**: Starts at -20°C, increases by 5°C every second until 135°C, then resets to -20°C
- **Turbo Pressure Gauge**: Cycles through 0.9-2.5 bar range in 0.1 bar steps every 1.5 seconds
- **Battery Voltage**: Cycles through 11.5V-16V in 0.25V steps every 2 seconds

### ✅ **How to Use:**

1. **Enable Display Test Mode:**
 - Uncomment `#define DISPLAY_TEST` in `include/config.h`

2. **Compile and Upload:**
 - Run `pio run` to compile
 - Upload to ESP32

3. **Test Results:**
 - Bluetooth functionality is disabled (no connection errors)
 - Display shows "DISPLAY TEST MODE" status
 - All gauges cycle through their respective ranges
 - Connection status shows if display is working properly

4. **Customization:**
 - All test parameters (ranges, steps, intervals) are configurable in `config.h`
 - Only included when `DISPLAY_TEST` is defined (conditional compilation)

### ✅ **Benefits:**

- **No Bluetooth Required**: Tests display functionality without needing OBD2 connection
- **Comprehensive Testing**: Tests all three gauge types (temperature, pressure, voltage)
- **Configurable Parameters**: Easy to adjust test ranges and timing
- **Production Safe**: Completely disabled when not needed (no code overhead)
- **Visual Confirmation**: Clear indication that display and gauges are working correctly

The implementation successfully provides a way to test the display functionality independently of the Bluetooth/OBD2 system, making it perfect for hardware validation and display testing scenarios.

First, commit all current changes to git with an appropriate commit message describing the display test functionality that was just implemented.

2. Then create a new feature branch called "obd2-diagnostic-scanner" or similar.

3. On this new branch, implement functionality where:
 - A physical button is connected to a GPIO pin on the ESP32
 - When the button is pressed, the system initiates an OBD2 diagnostic scan
 - The software queries all available OBD2 PIDs (Parameter IDs) that the connected vehicle supports
 - Instead of displaying the current gauge interface, switch to a text-based diagnostic display mode
 - Show each supported PID along with its current value and description on the TFT screen
 - Include common PIDs like engine RPM, vehicle speed, fuel level, engine load, throttle position, etc.
 - Display the results in a scrollable text format that cycles through all available parameters
 - Provide a way to exit this diagnostic mode and return to the normal gauge display

4. The implementation should:
 - Add button debouncing logic for reliable button press detection
 - Create a new display mode for showing diagnostic text data
 - Implement OBD2 PID discovery to find what the vehicle supports
 - Format the diagnostic data in a readable text format on the TFT display
 - Handle cases where certain PIDs are not supported by the vehicle","rich_text_json_repr":{"type":"doc","content":[{"type":"paragraph","content":[{"type":"text","text":"1. First, commit all current changes to git with an appropriate commit message describing the display test functionality that was just implemented.

2. Then create a new feature branch called "obd2-diagnostic-scanner" or similar.

3. On this new branch, implement functionality where:
 - A physical button is connected to a GPIO pin on the ESP32
 - When the button is pressed, the system initiates an OBD2 diagnostic scan
 - The software queries all available OBD2 PIDs (Parameter IDs) that the connected vehicle supports
 - Instead of displaying the current gauge interface, switch to a text-based diagnostic display mode
 - Show each supported PID along with its current value and description on the TFT screen
 - Include common PIDs like engine RPM, vehicle speed, fuel level, engine load, throttle position, etc.
 - Display the results in a scrollable text format that cycles through all available parameters
 - Provide a way to exit this diagnostic mode and return to the normal gauge display

4. The implementation should:
 - Add button debouncing logic for reliable button press detection
 - Create a new display mode for showing diagnostic text data
 - Implement OBD2 PID discovery to find what the vehicle supports

 29	 // Main application update loop
 30	 automotiveDisplay.update();
 31	
 32	 // Small delay to prevent excessive CPU usage
 33	 delay(DISPLAY_UPDATE_INTERVAL);
 34	}

 14	bool AutomotiveDisplay::begin() {
 15	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("Initializing AutomotiveDisplay...");
 16	 
 17	 // Initialize Serial for debugging
 18	 Serial.begin(DEBUG_SERIAL_BAUD);
 19	 delay(1000);
 20	 
 21	 DEBUG_PRINTLN(PROJECT_NAME " " PROJECT_VERSION);
 22	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("Build: " BUILD_DATE " " BUILD_TIME);
 23	 
 24	 // Initialize display first
 25	 if (!gaugeDisplay.begin()) {
 26	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("Failed to initialize display");
 27	 currentState = AppState::ERROR;

 29	 }
 30

 33	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("Failed to initialize OBD2 manager");
 34	 currentState = AppState::ERROR;
 35	 return false;
 36	 }
 37	 
 38	 // Show initial status
 39	 gaugeDisplay.showStatus("System Ready");
 40	 
 41	 currentState = AppState::WAITING_BT;
 42	 lastUpdate = millis();
 43	 lastStateCheck = millis();
 44	 
 45	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("AutomotiveDisplay initialization complete");
 46	 return true;
 47	}
 48	
 49	void AutomotiveDisplay::update() {
 50	 unsigned long currentTime = millis();
 51	 
 52	 // Update OBD2 manager state
 53	 obd2Manager.update();
 54	 
 55	 // Handle state transitions
 56	 if (currentTime - lastStateCheck >= STATE_CHECK_INTERVAL) {
 57	 handleStateTransitions();
 58	 lastStateCheck = currentTime;
 59	 }
 60	 
 61	 // Update sensor data and display
 62	 if (currentTime - lastUpdate >= UPDATE_INTERVAL) {
 63	 switch (currentState) {
 64	 case AppState::RUNNING:
 65	 updateSensorData();
 66	 updateDisplay();
 67	 break;
 68	 
 69	 case AppState::WAITING_BT:
 70	 gaugeDisplay.showStatus("Waiting for Bluetooth...");
 71	 break;
 72	 
 73	 case AppState::CONNECTING_OBD2:
 74	 gaugeDisplay.showStatus("Connecting to OBD2...");
 75	 break;
 76	 
 77	 case AppState::ERROR:
 78	 handleErrors();
 79	 break;
 80	 
 81	 default:
 82	 break;
 83	 }
 84	 
 85	 lastUpdate = currentTime;
 86	 }
 87	 
 88	 // Log debug info periodically
 89	 if (DEBUG_ENABLED && (currentTime % HEARTBEAT_INTERVAL == 0)) {
 90	 logDebugInfo();
 91	 }
 92	}
 93	
 94	String AutomotiveDisplay::getStatusString() const {
 95	 switch (currentState) {
 96	 case AppState::INITIALIZING: return "Initializing";
 97	 case AppState::WAITING_BT: return "Waiting for Bluetooth";
 98	 case AppState::CONNECTING_OBD2: return "Connecting to OBD2";
 99	 case AppState::RUNNING: return "Running";
 100	 case AppState::ERROR: return "Error";
 101	 default: return "Unknown";
 102	 }
 103	}
 104	
 105	void AutomotiveDisplay::handleStateTransitions() {
 106	 switch (currentState) {
 107	 case AppState::WAITING_BT:
 108	 if (obd2Manager.isConnected()) {
 109	 currentState = AppState::CONNECTING_OBD2;
 110	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("State: WAITING_BT -> CONNECTING_OBD2");
 111	 }
 112	 break;
 113	 
 114	 case AppState::CONNECTING_OBD2:
 115	 if (!obd2Manager.isConnected()) {
 116	 currentState = AppState::WAITING_BT;
 117	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("State: CONNECTING_OBD2 -> WAITING_BT");
 118	 } else if (obd2Manager.isReady()) {
 119	 currentState = AppState::RUNNING;
 120	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("State: CONNECTING_OBD2 -> RUNNING");
 121	 gaugeDisplay.showStatus("OBD2 Connected");
 122	 }
 123	 break;
 124	 
 125	 case AppState::RUNNING:
 126	 if (!obd2Manager.isConnected()) {
 127	 currentState = AppState::WAITING_BT;
 128	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("State: RUNNING -> WAITING_BT");
 129	 currentSensorData.invalidate();
 130	 } else if (!obd2Manager.isReady()) {
 131	 currentState = AppState::CONNECTING_OBD2;
 132	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("State: RUNNING -> CONNECTING_OBD2");
 133	 }
 134	 break;
 135	 
 136	 case AppState::ERROR:
 137	 // Try to recover after delay
 138	 if (millis() - lastStateCheck > ERROR_RETRY_INTERVAL) {
 139	 currentState = AppState::INITIALIZING;
 140	 DEBUG_PRINTLN("State: ERROR -> INITIALIZING (retry)");
 141	 begin(); // Reinitialize
 142	 }
 143	 break;
 144	 
 145	 default:
 146	 break;
 147	 }
 148	}
 149	
 150	void AutomotiveDisplay::updateSensorData() {
 151	 if (obd2Manager.isReady()) {
 152	 const SensorData newData = obd2Manager.requestSensorData();
 153	 if (newData.isValid) {
 154	 currentSensorData = newData;
 155	 DEBUG_PRINTF("Sensor data updated: Temp=%.1f°C, Boost=%.2fbar\n", 
 156	 newData.coolantTemperature, newData.boostPressure);
 157	 }
 158	 }
 159	}
 160	
 161	void AutomotiveDisplay::updateDisplay() {
 162	 if (currentSensorData.isValid) {
 163	 gaugeDisplay.updateGauges(currentSensorData);
 164	 
 165	 // Check for critical values and update status
 166	 String status = "Normal";
 167	 if (currentSensorData.coolantTemperature > TEMP_CRITICAL_VALUE) {
 168	 status = "HIGH TEMP WARNING!";
 169	 } else if (currentSensorData.boostPressure > BOOST_CRITICAL_VALUE) {
 170	 status = "HIGH BOOST WARNING!";
 171	 }
 172	 #if BATTERY_VOLTAGE_ENABLED
 173	 else if (currentSensorData.batteryVoltageValid) {
 174	 if (currentSensorData.batteryVoltage < VOLTAGE_LOW_THRESHOLD) {
 175	 status = "LOW BATTERY WARNING!";
 176	 } else if (currentSensorData.batteryVoltage > VOLTAGE_HIGH_THRESHOLD) {
 177	 status = "HIGH VOLTAGE WARNING!";
 178	 }
 179	 }
 180	 #endif
 181	
 182	 if (status != "Normal") {
 183	 gaugeDisplay.showStatus(status);
 184	 }

 6
